title: Kitam bird sanctuary
tagline: Kitam bird sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Sikkim

overview.short: Kitam Bird Sanctuary is a protected area and wildlife sanctuary located about 19 km (12 mi) Namchi in the Namchi district of the Indian state of Sikkim.
overview.description: Kitam Bird Sanctuary is a protected area and wildlife sanctuary located about 19 km (12 mi) Namchi in the Namchi district of the Indian state of Sikkim. It was declared as a protected area on 3 February 2005.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kitam bird sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kitam bird sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kitam bird sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pakyong Airport, Gangtok (29 km) and the nearest railway station is Siliguri Junction (44 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kitam bird sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kitam bird sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kitam bird sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Kitam bird sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kitam bird sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Glenburn Tea Estate, Manjhitar, Badamtam Tea Garden, Badamtam Tea Estate, Singla Tea Garden.
